name: Circular gravel route through the Desierto de Tabernas
name_original: Ruta gravel circular por el Desierto de Tabernas
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Tabernas traverse Europe's only true desert, characterized by its distinctive "badlands" topography. This landscape features dramatically eroded ravines, dry riverbeds known as ramblas, and barren, moon-like slopes. The region's semi-arid Mediterranean climate and geological history as an ancient seabed have sculpted its unique terrain, nestled between the Sierra de los Filabres, Sierra Alhamilla, and Sierra Nevada mountain ranges. Despite its arid appearance, the desert supports a unique ecosystem and is designated as a Protected Natural Area. Best touring cycling routes around Tabernas The most popular touring cycling route is Circular gravel route through the Desierto de Tabernas, a 10.4 miles (16.7 km) trail that takes 1 hour 24 minutes to complete. This route offers a direct experience of the desert's gravel terrain. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Tabernas Ravine – Texas Hollywood loop from Tabernas, a moderate 16.4 miles (26.4 km) path. This route explores the Tabernas Ravine and passes by the famous Texas Hollywood film set. Local touring cyclists also love the Tabernas Village and Desert – Alto de Velefique Pass loop from Tabernas, a 38.8 miles (62.5 km) trail leading through the Tabernas Desert and ascending to the Alto de Velefique Pass, often completed in about 4 hours 34 minutes. Touring cycling around Tabernas is defined by its badlands topography, dry riverbeds (ramblas), and arid desert landscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy rides to challenging routes with significant elevation gains. The routes in Tabernas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.1 stars from more than 50 reviews. More than 500 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Tabernas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Tabernas?
A: Tabernas offers a diverse network of over 45 touring cycling routes. These routes cater to various skill levels, ranging from easy rides to challenging expeditions with significant elevation gains.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Tabernas?
A: The terrain in Tabernas is uniquely characterized by its 'badlands' topography, featuring dramatically eroded ravines, dry riverbeds known as ramblas, and barren, moon-like slopes. While some routes are on mostly paved surfaces, many explore gravel paths and unpaved segments, offering a true desert cycling experience.

Q: Are there routes suitable for beginners or families in Tabernas?
A: Yes, Tabernas has options for less experienced cyclists and families. There are 4 easy routes and 13 moderate routes available. These often feature gentler gradients and more stable surfaces, allowing for a comfortable exploration of the desert landscape.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Tabernas?
A: The most popular months for cycling in Tabernas are April and October. During these periods, the weather is generally pleasant, offering ideal conditions for exploring the desert without the extreme heat of summer or the cooler temperatures of winter.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes in Tabernas?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in Tabernas are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1046231482/circular-gravel-route-through-the-desierto-de-tabernas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circular gravel route through the Desierto de Tabernas&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a direct experience of the desert's gravel terrain.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions I can see along the touring cycling routes?
A: The Tabernas Desert is rich in unique sights. You can cycle past famous film sets like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4599892" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Texas Hollywood&lt;/a&gt;, explore the rugged &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5540654" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tabernas Ravine&lt;/a&gt;, or even spot the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/635124" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tabernas Oasis&lt;/a&gt;. Many routes also offer panoramic views of the surrounding 'badlands' topography.

Q: Are there any challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. Tabernas offers 31 difficult touring cycling routes for those seeking a challenge. These routes often feature significant elevation gains and cover longer distances, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20119169" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tabernas Village and Desert – Alto de Velefique Pass loop from Tabernas&lt;/a&gt;, which ascends to a high mountain pass.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Tabernas?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 50 reviews. Reviewers often praise the unique desert landscape, the variety of routes for different skill levels, and the opportunity to explore a region famous for its cinematic history.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by the famous film sets?
A: Yes, several routes incorporate the iconic film sets that give the Tabernas Desert its 'Wild West' feel. For instance, the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31860062" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tabernas Ravine – Texas Hollywood loop from Tabernas&lt;/a&gt; explores the Tabernas Ravine and passes directly by the famous Texas Hollywood film set.

Q: Can I find routes that offer views of the wider desert landscape?
A: Many routes provide excellent viewpoints to appreciate the vastness and unique geology of the Tabernas Desert. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20120778" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tabernas Village and Desert – Texas Hollywood loop from Circuito Costa de Almería&lt;/a&gt; offers expansive views as it traverses the desert landscape.

Q: Are there any routes that explore areas outside the immediate Tabernas village?
A: Yes, some routes venture further afield, allowing you to discover more of the surrounding region. An example is the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38174795" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lucainena de las Torres loop from Turrillas&lt;/a&gt;, which explores different villages and landscapes adjacent to the Tabernas Desert.
tips:
Tabernas is a town in the Los Filabres - Tabernas region, in the province of Almería. It is located next to the Tabernas Desert Natural Park, a unique desert area in Europe.

This area has great scenic and geological value, and boasts its own unique flora and fauna. It is precisely these landscapes that made this area an important open-air film set where numerous Westerns and other films simulating African and Arabian locations were filmed.

Tabernas already had an Almohad castle in the 10th century, given its location on the communication route between Almería and the eastern Iberian Peninsula. After the expulsion of the Moors, the castle was almost in ruins and was rebuilt by Emperor Charles V.

Ford Bravo is a recreated Western village. The buildings in the village appear in various western films. When SERGIO Leone discovered Tabernas in the early 1960s, a wave of recordings started. Many film recordings were made in the beautiful desert environment.
